# Project blueprint

<!-- mol:map:managed begin -->
<!-- This block is regenerated by /mol:map. Custom annotations go
     OUTSIDE these markers and are preserved across re-runs. -->

_Generated 2026-05-08 by /mol:map._

## Inventory

The repository at `/Users/roykid/work/molcrafts/molnex/` is laid out as four
sibling packages under `src/` (`molix`, `molpot`, `molrep`, `molzoo`), each
with sub-packages exposing a curated public surface via `__init__.py`
re-exports. Mirror tests live under `tests/test_<package>/test_<subpkg>/`.

## molix — Training infrastructure

### molix (top-level)
- Modules: __init__.py, config.py, compile.py, logging.py, logger.py
- Public surface: Stage, TrainState, StepResult, Trainer, Checkpoint,
  CheckpointBackend, TorchSaveBackend, MSELoss, MAELoss, WeightedLoss,
  config, logger, logging, maybe_compile, ProfilerHook
- Style: stdlib-compatible logging facade with channels (metrics/events/
  info/warn); eagerly loads C++ ops at import; raises build instructions
  on miss
- Layer: foundation tier of the Module Dependency Graph;
  molix.config = global dtype singleton

### molix.core
- Modules: state.py, trainer.py, hooks.py, metrics.py, protocols.py,
  checkpoint/, losses/, steps/
- Public surface: TrainState/Stage/StepResult/resolve/display; Trainer;
  Hook + BaseHook + ScalarHook + 12 concrete hooks (TensorBoardHook,
  CheckpointHook, ProgressBarHook, MetricsHook, StepSpeedHook,
  ProfilerHook, GradClipHook, ActivationCheckpointingHook, GPUMemoryHook,
  GPUUtilsHook, Log); Metric/BaseMetric/MAE/RMSE/MSE/R2Score/Accuracy/
  MetricCollection; Checkpoint + backends + RNG capture/restore; losses;
  Step/DefaultTrainStep/DefaultEvalStep/extract_model_inputs/batch_to_device
- Style: hook namespace contract (writes only into state["train"|"eval"|
  "performance"|"gpu"], flat/tuple writes raise); homegrown metrics
  (NOT a torchmetrics dep — `molix.core.metrics` defines its own MAE,
  RMSE, MSE, R2Score, Accuracy, satisfying a Metric Protocol that is
  shape-compatible with torchmetrics but does not import it); deepcopy
  across train/val to prevent buffer cross-talk
- Layer: top execution layer of CLAUDE.md's Module Dependency Graph
- NAMING CONVENTION (in transition): `metric-recorder` spec lands a
  new hook `Journal` under an instrument-noun convention (drop `Hook`
  suffix; name after the instrument/artifact: ProgressBar, Speedometer,
  GPUMemoryGauge, Scoreboard, Dashboard, Journal, TensorBoard,
  Checkpointer, Profiler, GradClipper). Existing 10 `*Hook` classes
  remain under the legacy convention until a follow-up `hook-naming`
  refactor spec migrates them. Coexistence is intentional during the
  migration window.

### molix.data
- Modules: types.py, source.py, task.py, tasks/, pipeline.py, cache.py,
  dataset.py, datamodule.py, collate.py
- Public surface: AtomData/EdgeData/GraphData/GraphBatch; DataSource +
  InMemorySource + SubsetSource; Task/SampleTask/DatasetTask/BatchTask/
  Runnable; AtomicDress/ConstantLabel/NeighborList/UnitConvert; Pipeline/
  PipelineSpec/TaskEntry; PackedCache; BaseDataset/MmapDataset/
  CachedDataset/SubsetDataset/split_indices; DataModule/DataModuleProtocol;
  collate_molecules/TargetSchema
- Style: two-tier data contract (flat-dict pre-collate, nested TensorDict
  post-collate); OOP encapsulation per feedback_oop_style — no free-
  function pipeline.cache exposure; PackedCache deliberately rejects
  TensorDict.memmap_() (HPC inode rationale documented in cache.py)
- Layer: data layer — feeds into Trainer; collate boundary is the
  flat→nested handoff

### molix.datasets
- Modules: qm9.py, revmd17.py, threebpa.py
- Public surface: QM9Source, RevMD17Source, ThreeBPASource (each with
  TARGET_SCHEMA + download() classmethod)
- Style: each source is a class with class-attr TARGET_SCHEMA + download
  classmethod; private parser helpers
- Layer: concrete data source layer; consumed by Pipeline to materialise
  PackedCache files

### molix.nn
- Modules: locality.py, mlp.py, scatter.py
- Public surface: NeighborList, KeyedMLP/KeyedMLPSpec, ScatterSum,
  BatchAggregation
- Style: nn.Module wrappers around molix.F functional primitives;
  Pydantic Spec dataclasses
- Layer: stateful nn.Module layer between molix.F and molrep/molzoo

### molix.F
- Modules: locality.py, pot.py, scatter.py
- Public surface: get_neighbor_pairs, pme_direct, pme_reciprocal,
  scatter_sum, batch_add
- Style: stateless functional API over torch.ops.molix C++ kernels +
  torch-native scatter primitives
- Layer: lowest-level functional layer — wraps molix.op C++ ops

### molix.op
- Modules: CMakeLists.txt, accessor.cuh, molix_library.cpp,
  molix_meta.cpp, neighbors/get_neighbor_pairs.{cpp,cu}, pme/pme_*.{cpp,cu}
- Public surface: torch.ops.molix::get_neighbor_pairs / pme_direct /
  pme_reciprocal (registered into PyTorch dispatcher)
- Style: single CMake target, CPU/CUDA backends; ops registered via
  TORCH_LIBRARY; meta-tensor implementations registered separately
- Layer: native compute kernel layer — only place C++/CUDA appears

### molix.profiler
- Modules: _utils.py, task.py, module.py, dataloader.py, mock.py
- Public surface: TaskProfiler/ModuleProfiler/DataLoaderProfiler +
  Result classes; MockBatch/MockSource
- Style: OOP profiler suite; .run() returns Result dataclass with
  print_report(); CUDA timer via torch.cuda.Event
- Layer: diagnostic side-tier — never imported by training code

### molix.recorder (planned, lands via metric-recorder spec)
- Modules: schema.py, zarr_store.py, reader.py
- Public surface: MetricRecord (frozen dataclass), MetricStore (Protocol;
  append/flush/close only — no update/delete by design), ZarrMetricStore
  (concrete v3 backend with sharding), MetricsReader
- Style: append-only record stream conforming to molrec spec §metrics;
  Zarr v3 sharded layout (1024-row chunks, 1M-row shards) for the same
  inode-discipline reason that motivated PackedCache; semantic-only
  dependency on molrec — does NOT import molcrafts-molrs
- Layer: persistence side-tier — read by `Journal` hook on the write
  side; consumed by external analysis on the read side

## molpot — Potential functions

### molpot.potentials
- Modules: base.py, lj126.py, bond_harmonic.py, angle_harmonic.py,
  dihedral_harmonic.py, nonbonded.py, polarization.py, mixing.py
- Public surface: BasePotential; LJ126/lorentz_berthelot;
  BondHarmonic/AngleHarmonic/DihedralHarmonic; RepulsionExp6/DispersionC6/
  ChargeTransfer; Polarization; geometric_arithmetic_mixing
- Style: BasePotential = nn.Module + ABC; forward() returns scalar
  energy; calc_forces() via torch.autograd.grad; each potential carries
  its own mixing_fn for per-atom → per-pair param expansion
- Layer: concrete potential terms — second-to-top of molpot tier

### molpot.heads
- Modules: heads.py, edge_energy.py, multipole.py, rescale.py
- Public surface: AtomicEnergyMLP/EnergyHead/TypeHead/EdgeEnergyHead;
  PermMultipoleHead + Spec + VALID_DAMPINGS/VALID_ENERGY_TERMS;
  GlobalRescale/PerSpeciesScaleShift
- Style: plain nn.Module heads; Pydantic Spec dataclasses
- Layer: prediction-head layer — feeds PotentialComposer

### molpot.composition
- Modules: composer.py, heads.py, multi_head.py, pooling.py
- Public surface: PotentialComposer; LJParameterHead/RepulsionParameterHead/
  ChargeTransferParameterHead/ChargeHead/TSScalingHead; MultiHead
- Style: PotentialComposer is a thin nn.Module orchestrator —
  head → params → potential.mixing_fn → energy → sum + optional autograd
  forces
- Layer: PotentialComposer tier — bridges encoder output and potentials

### molpot.derivation
- Modules: energy_aggregation.py, force_derivation.py, stress_derivation.py
- Public surface: EnergyAggregation, ForceDerivation, StressDerivation
- Style: each is nn.Module; force/stress use torch.autograd.grad with
  create_graph=self.training
- Layer: autograd forces / virial — physical-quantity derivation between
  heads and loss

### molpot.pooling
- Modules: layer_pooling.py, edge_to_node.py, graph_pooling.py
- Public surface: LayerPooling, EdgeToNodePooling, SumPooling/MeanPooling/
  MaxPooling
- Style: plain nn.Module adapters for encoder-output → atom/edge/graph
  reductions
- Layer: adapter layer between molzoo encoders and molpot composer/heads

### molpot.graph
- Modules: radius_graph.py
- Public surface: radius_graph
- Style: single utility free function — edge-index from positions + cutoff
- Layer: graph-construction utility — molpot-side analogue of
  molix.nn.locality

## molrep — Representation learning

### molrep.embedding
- Modules: node.py, angular.py, radial.py, cutoff.py, scalar_mlp.py
- Public surface: JointEmbedding, SphericalHarmonics, BesselRBF,
  CosineCutoff, PolynomialCutoff
- Style: equivariant embeddings on cuequivariance/cuequivariance_torch;
  Pydantic Spec construction; SphericalHarmonics consumes
  bond_diff = pos[target] − pos[source] per edge convention
- Layer: Embedding stage of the embedding → interaction → readout pipeline

### molrep.interaction
- Modules: tensor_product.py, aggregation.py, element_update.py, linear.py,
  symmetric_contraction.py, radial_mlp.py
- Public surface: MessageAggregation, EquivariantLinear, ConvTP,
  SymmetricContraction, ElementUpdate, RadialWeightMLP +
  irreps_from_l_max/sh_irreps_from_l_max
- Style: nn.Module + Pydantic Spec; tensor-product convolutions use
  cuequivariance Irreps + EquivariantPolynomialTP; aggregation uses
  scatter-sum on edge_index[:,1] (target) per edge convention
- Layer: Interaction stage of the pipeline

### molrep.readout
- Modules: product_head.py, basis_projection.py, pooling.py
- Public surface: ProductHead, BasisProjection, masked_sum_pooling,
  masked_mean_pooling, ScatterPooling
- Style: nn.Module + Pydantic Spec; pooling pair as free functions, plus
  stateful ScatterPooling counterpart
- Layer: Readout stage; physical-quantity heads (energy/force/stress) are
  in molpot, not here

### molrep.head
- Modules: scalar_head.py, labeler.py, type_head.py
- Public surface: ScalarHead, TypeHead, Labeler, ProxyLabeler
- Style: generic post-readout heads; plain nn.Module
- Layer: auxiliary heads — complementary to molpot.heads (which are
  physically-typed)

### molrep.utils
- Modules: equivariance.py, geometry.py
- Public surface: NeighborGraphBuilder, SphericalBasis, GaussianRBF,
  CosineCutoff (+ rotation helpers used in tests)
- Style: free-function helpers; geometry duplicates a non-equivariant
  fallback subset
- Layer: utility tier — used by tests + non-cuEquivariance code paths

## molzoo — Pre-built encoders

### molzoo (top-level)
- Modules: mace.py, allegro.py, symmetry_helpers.py, utils.py, README.md
- Public surface: MACE, MACESpec, Allegro, AllegroSpec — accept
  (Z, bond_dist, bond_diff, edge_index) → (N, num_layers, num_features)
- Style: TensorDictModuleBase subclass per encoder; Pydantic Spec; module
  docstring carries paper DOI/arXiv per the Scientific Correctness rule;
  Allegro uses cuEquivariance descriptors, MACE composes molrep blocks
- Layer: encoder-only tier — produces raw features; readout/potentials
  are downstream in molpot

### molzoo.specs
- Modules: allegro.md (only spec currently checked in)
- Public surface: per-encoder spec markdown, 10 immutable sections (Scope/
  Paper↔Code Mapping/Reference Alignment/Adaptation Ledger/Math Contract/
  Config Mapping/Benchmark Contract incl. §7.4 run log/System Boundary/
  Version Pinning/Drift Policy)
- Style: append-only §7.4 run log; authored by /molzoo-spec; audited by
  molzoo-auditor (📝/🆚 patch spec, ⚠️ print to chat); status
  draft→partial→ready gates code authoring
- Layer: documentation/contract layer paired one-to-one with each encoder
- KNOWN GAP: mace.md is absent despite mace.py existing — violates the
  "one spec per encoder" rule in CLAUDE.md

## Tests

- Mirrors src/ tree: tests/test_molix/{test_core,test_data,test_datasets,
  test_nn}, tests/test_molpot/{test_composition,test_heads,test_potentials},
  tests/test_molrep/{test_embedding,test_interaction,test_readout},
  tests/test_molzoo/test_{allegro,mace,mace_encoder,symmetry}.py
- Pytest with parametrised tests + conftest fixtures; symmetry/
  equivariance checks in tests/test_molzoo/test_symmetry.py
- CI runs CPU-only pytest job (no coverage gate)
